input_messages.content: {"suggestions": ["Configure egress firewall policy on FortiGate to block outbound ICMP traffic", "Apply an outbound Access Control List on cisco-router-R2 to block ICMP traffic from the 192.168.2.0/24 subnet", "Implement rate-limiting for all outbound ICMP traffic on the FortiGate firewall", "Apply host-based firewall rules on UbuntuComputer to block all outgoing ICMP traffic", "Deploy Snort IPS inline between cisco-router-R2 and the FortiGate firewall to block the ICMP flood"], "chosen_mitigation": "Configure egress firewall policy on FortiGate to block outbound ICMP traffic", "no_mitigation_left": false, "current_mitigations_counter": 1, "reached_mitigation_limit": false}

```json
{
  "mitigation_currently_implemented": "Configure egress firewall policy on FortiGate to block outbound ICMP traffic",
  "comments": "An egress firewall policy was implemented on the FortiGate device to block all outbound ICMP (ping) traffic. The attack originates from the UbuntuComputer, which sends a flood of ICMP packets. By identifying the correct interfaces (port3 for internal and port1 for external) and the correct service name ('PING'), a firewall policy was created. This rule (policy ID 4) denies any PING service traffic from the internal network to the external network, effectively stopping the ICMP flood attack from reaching its target.",
  "device_identifier": "fortigate-firewall",
  "commands": [
    "config firewall policy
edit 4
set name "Block-ICMP-Egress-4"
set srcintf port3
set dstintf port1
set srcaddr all
set dstaddr all
set action deny
set service PING
set schedule always
set status enable
end"
  ]
}
```

```json
{
  "mitigation_currently_implemented": "Apply an outbound Access Control List on cisco-router-R2 to block ICMP traffic from the 192.168.2.0/24 subnet",
  "comments": "I have successfully implemented an outbound Access Control List (ACL) on the 'cisco-router-R2' device. This ACL, numbered 101, is configured to deny all ICMP traffic originating from the 192.168.2.0/24 subnet, which includes the compromised 'UbuntuComputer'. The ACL is applied to the FastEthernet1/0 interface in the outbound direction, effectively blocking the ICMP flood at the network edge before it can reach the FortiGate firewall or the internet.",
  "device_identifier": "cisco-router-R2",
  "commands": [
    "configure terminal",
    "access-list 101 deny icmp 192.168.2.0 0.0.0.255 any",
    "access-list 101 permit ip any any",
    "interface fastethernet1/0",
    "ip access-group 101 out",
    "end"
  ]
}
```

Applying this list to the outbound interface fastethernet1/0, which connects towards the firewall, is the appropriate place to stop the traffic. The inclusion of 'access-list 101 permit ip any any' is a critical step that was correctly included to prevent blocking all other legitimate traffic. The implementation is approved.", "approved": true, "mitigation_currently_implemented": "Apply an outbound Access Control List on cisco-router-R2 to block ICMP traffic from the 192.168.2.0/24 subnet", "mitigations_counter": 3, "mitigation_iteration": 1, "reached_mitigation_iteration_limit": false}
